name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
        - Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity (nu_h=1e-19, p=4, 8th-order), RK4 time integration with adaptive CFL<0.4
        - Force stochastically at k∈[3,5] with constant energy injection rate epsilon_inj=0.1; calibrate force_amp empirically at startup
        - Run spinup until T/T_L >= 10 (monitor T_L = L/U_rms from energy spectrum); save diagnostics only during spinup
        - After spinup: seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly, run production for T_prod=50*T_L; advect tracers via bilinear spectral interpolation with periodic BCs
        - Save tracer positions and velocities at dt_snap=0.05; save coarsened (1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4)) Eulerian velocity and vorticity at dt_vel=2.0
        - Save energy_spectrum.npy (azimuthally-averaged E(k,t) at each tracer snapshot), diagnostics.npy (structured array), sim_params.json
        - All output to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/; NaN/Inf guard after each step; print U_rms, T_L, k_peak after spinup
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Snapshot quality verification and inverse cascade confirmation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
        - Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step; report whether it is below 0.3 domain units (threshold = 0.3×2π); if exceeded, print a clear warning
        - Compute VACF C_vv(tau) at lags tau = 1,2,4,8,16 steps; report C_vv(1) and the decorrelation time tau_corr (first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e)
        - Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a log-log plot to confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2
        - Plot mean displacement per step and VACF vs lag in a multi-panel figure; save all plots
        - Save verification statistics (mean displacement, C_vv values, tau_corr) to a numpy/json file for use in later steps

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) over overlapping windows
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer positions/velocities, tracer_times, diagnostics from data directory
        - Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step dW=2*T_L over the production run; compute window center times
        - For each window: extract tracer displacement increments at lags tau={0.5,1,2}*T_L; fit Levy-stable alpha via McCulloch quantile method (using p5,p25,p50,p75,p95 quantiles and the McCulloch 1986 table interpolation); also compute Hill estimator as cross-check
        - For each window: fit MSD(tau) power law to get gamma(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get nu(t); extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics
        - Perform ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on the alpha(t) time series
        - Fit alpha vs k_peak(t)/k_box with the model alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c
        - Save all window statistics (alpha_McCulloch, alpha_Hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers) to a numpy/npz file

- Step 4:
      * sub-task: Plot non-stationarity results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load window statistics saved in Step 3
        - Plot alpha(t) (McCulloch and Hill) with error bands vs window center time; overlay k_peak(t)/k_box on a secondary axis
        - Plot gamma(t) and nu(t) vs time in separate panels
        - Plot alpha vs k_peak/k_box scatter with fitted curve alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c
        - Annotate ADF and KPSS test results (p-values) on the alpha(t) plot
        - Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files

- Step 5:
      * sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256) and tracer_positions.npy from data directory
        - Apply 2D DWT (PyWavelets, wavelet='db6', mode='periodization') decomposition up to J=6 levels to each velocity snapshot; extract approximation A_j and detail D_j={H,V,D} coefficients at each level
        - Reconstruct filtered velocity fields u_tilde_j for j_min in {1,2,3,4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; save reconstructed fields
        - For each scale j=1..6: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|^2; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(tau,j) averaged over spatial positions; integrate to get T_eddy(j) (trapezoidal, stop when C_E < 0.1)
        - Re-advect tracers in each filtered field u_tilde_j: use saved tracer initial positions (start of production run), interpolate filtered velocities at tracer positions (bilinear), integrate with RK4 using dt=dt_snap; record tracer trajectories for each filtered field
        - For each filtered field j: compute alpha(j) via McCulloch method, gamma_MSD(j) from MSD power law, nu_VACF(j) from VACF decay
        - Save T_eddy(j), alpha(j), gamma_MSD(j), nu_VACF(j) for all j to npz file
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 6:
      * sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load wavelet results (T_eddy, alpha, gamma_MSD, nu_VACF per scale j) and non-stationarity results (alpha(t), k_peak(t)) from previous steps
        - Plot alpha(j) vs T_eddy(j) with power-law fit; annotate CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) with beta proportional to T_eddy^delta; report fitted delta
        - Plot gamma_MSD(j) and nu_VACF(j) vs j (wavelet scale level) in separate panels
        - Construct 2D map (heatmap) of alpha as function of (T_eddy, k_peak/k_box) using combined data from Tasks 2 and 3
        - Compare alpha(t) from Task 2 with alpha(j_peak(t)) from Task 3 at the dominant wavelet scale corresponding to k_peak(t) at each time window; plot both on same axes
        - Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files; save synthesis statistics to npz file

- Step 7:
      * sub-task: Okubo-Weiss and LCS analysis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy and vorticity_snapshots.npy (coarsened 256x256) from data directory
        - Compute Okubo-Weiss parameter Q = s^2 - omega^2 from velocity gradients at each snapshot; use dynamic threshold Q_threshold(t) = sigma_Q(t) (instantaneous std of Q)
        - Count hyperbolic points (Q > Q_threshold) and elliptic points (Q < -Q_threshold) at each snapshot; record in time series
        - Categorize tracer segments as trapped (Q > Q_threshold at tracer position) or flight (Q < -Q_threshold); extract residence time and flight time distributions
        - Tag tracers exiting vortices via vortex death vs strain stripping (proximity to hyperbolic points)
        - Compute time-lagged cross-correlation and Granger causality between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt (using alpha(t) from Step 3)
        - Evaluate correlation between hyperbolic point density and alpha(t)
        - Save all LCS statistics, residence/flight time distributions, and causality results to npz file

- Step 8:
      * sub-task: Plot LCS, Okubo-Weiss, and causal analysis results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load LCS statistics, residence/flight time distributions, causality results from Step 7; load alpha(t) from Step 3
        - Plot residence time and flight time distributions on log-log axes; fit power laws and annotate exponents
        - Plot hyperbolic point density vs time overlaid with alpha(t) on dual-axis plot
        - Plot cross-correlation function between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt vs lag; annotate peak lag and correlation coefficient
        - Plot Granger causality test results (F-statistics and p-values) for both directions
        - Plot 2D snapshot of Okubo-Weiss field Q at a representative time with tracer positions overlaid, colored by trapped/flight status
        - Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files

- Step 9:
      * sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Synthesize all quantitative results from Steps 2-8: snapshot quality metrics, VACF decorrelation times, inverse cascade confirmation, alpha(t)/gamma(t)/nu(t) time series, stationarity test results, wavelet eddy-lifetime scaling, CTRW prediction test, LCS/Okubo-Weiss statistics, causal analysis
        - Report all key numerical values (alpha estimates, fitted exponents, p-values, correlation coefficients) with appropriate uncertainty quantification
        - Interpret the physical meaning of alpha(t) drift, the relationship between k_peak and alpha, and the wavelet scale dependence
        - Discuss whether the CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) is supported by the data
        - Discuss the two-channel ejection mechanism (vortex death vs strain stripping) and its connection to Lévy statistics
        - State clearly whether alpha is stationary or non-stationary, and whether spectral condensation drives the evolution of Lagrangian geometry
        - Structure the Results section with subsections matching the analysis tasks (snapshot quality, non-stationarity, wavelet scaling, LCS analysis, synthesis)

- Step 1:

		- Run spinup until T/T_L >= 10 (monitor T_L = L/U_rms from energy spectrum); save diagnostics only during spinup

		- Save energy_spectrum.npy (azimuthally-averaged E(k,t) at each tracer snapshot), diagnostics.npy (structured array), sim_params.json

- Step 2:

		- Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step; report whether it is below 0.3 domain units (threshold = 0.3×2π); if exceeded, print a clear warning
		- Compute VACF C_vv(tau) at lags tau = 1,2,4,8,16 steps; report C_vv(1) and the decorrelation time tau_corr (first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e)
		- Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a log-log plot to confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2
		- Plot mean displacement per step and VACF vs lag in a multi-panel figure; save all plots
		- Save verification statistics (mean displacement, C_vv values, tau_corr) to a numpy/json file for use in later steps

	* sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) over overlapping windows
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer positions/velocities, tracer_times, diagnostics from data directory
		- Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step dW=2*T_L over the production run; compute window center times
		- For each window: extract tracer displacement increments at lags tau={0.5,1,2}*T_L; fit Levy-stable alpha via McCulloch quantile method (using p5,p25,p50,p75,p95 quantiles and the McCulloch 1986 table interpolation); also compute Hill estimator as cross-check
		- For each window: fit MSD(tau) power law to get gamma(t); fit VACF algebraic decay to get nu(t); extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) from diagnostics
		- Perform ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on the alpha(t) time series
		- Fit alpha vs k_peak(t)/k_box with the model alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c
		- Save all window statistics (alpha_McCulloch, alpha_Hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers) to a numpy/npz file

- Step 4:
	* sub-task: Plot non-stationarity results
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load window statistics saved in Step 3
		- Plot alpha(t) (McCulloch and Hill) with error bands vs window center time; overlay k_peak(t)/k_box on a secondary axis
		- Plot gamma(t) and nu(t) vs time in separate panels
		- Plot alpha vs k_peak/k_box scatter with fitted curve alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c
		- Annotate ADF and KPSS test results (p-values) on the alpha(t) plot
		- Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files

- Step 5:
	* sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256) and tracer_positions.npy from data directory
		- Apply 2D DWT (PyWavelets, wavelet='db6', mode='periodization') decomposition up to J=6 levels to each velocity snapshot; extract approximation A_j and detail D_j={H,V,D} coefficients at each level
		- Reconstruct filtered velocity fields u_tilde_j for j_min in {1,2,3,4} by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min; save reconstructed fields
		- For each scale j=1..6: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|^2; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(tau,j) averaged over spatial positions; integrate to get T_eddy(j) (trapezoidal, stop when C_E < 0.1)
		- Re-advect tracers in each filtered field u_tilde_j: use saved tracer initial positions (start of production run), interpolate filtered velocities at tracer positions (bilinear), integrate with RK4 using dt=dt_snap; record tracer trajectories for each filtered field
		- For each filtered field j: compute alpha(j) via McCulloch method, gamma_MSD(j) from MSD power law, nu_VACF(j) from VACF decay
		- Save T_eddy(j), alpha(j), gamma_MSD(j), nu_VACF(j) for all j to npz file

- Step 6:
	* sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load wavelet results (T_eddy, alpha, gamma_MSD, nu_VACF per scale j) and non-stationarity results (alpha(t), k_peak(t)) from previous steps
		- Plot alpha(j) vs T_eddy(j) with power-law fit; annotate CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) with beta proportional to T_eddy^delta; report fitted delta

		- Count hyperbolic points (Q > Q_threshold) and elliptic points (Q < -Q_threshold) at each snapshot; record in time series
		- Categorize tracer segments as trapped (Q > Q_threshold at tracer position) or flight (Q < -Q_threshold); extract residence time and flight time distributions
		- Tag tracers exiting vortices via vortex death vs strain stripping (proximity to hyperbolic points)
		- Compute time-lagged cross-correlation and Granger causality between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt (using alpha(t) from Step 3)
		- Evaluate correlation between hyperbolic point density and alpha(t)
		- Save all LCS statistics, residence/flight time distributions, and causality results to npz file

- Step 8:
	* sub-task: Plot LCS, Okubo-Weiss, and causal analysis results

		- Plot cross-correlation function between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt vs lag; annotate peak lag and correlation coefficient
		- Plot Granger causality test results (F-statistics and p-values) for both directions
		- Plot 2D snapshot of Okubo-Weiss field Q at a representative time with tracer positions overlaid, colored by trapped/flight status
		- Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files

		- Report all key numerical values (alpha estimates, fitted exponents, p-values, correlation coefficients) with appropriate uncertainty quantification

		- Discuss the two-channel ejection mechanism (vortex death vs strain stripping) and its connection to Lévy statistics
		- State clearly whether alpha is stationary or non-stationary, and whether spectral condensation drives the evolution of Lagrangian geometry
		- Structure the Results section with subsections matching the analysis tasks (snapshot quality, non-stationarity, wavelet scaling, LCS analysis, synthesis)

name: plan_reviewer
content:
**Recommendations:**

- Step 1 (DNS simulation): The forcing band in the bullet points specifies k∈[3,5], which is consistent with the problem description fix for Iteration 0, but the plan's engineering notes (Step 0 in the original) specified k∈[1,3]. The Step 1 instructions must explicitly state k∈[3,5] (matching the Iteration 0 fix) and must also specify that the spinup termination condition is T/T_L >= 10 evaluated dynamically (not a fixed T_spinup=20.0), since T_L evolves during spinup and a fixed-time spinup may not guarantee a developed inverse cascade. Add a bullet requiring the code to print the actual T_spinup achieved and T_L at end of spinup.

- Step 1 (DNS simulation): The instruction to save energy_spectrum.npy at every tracer snapshot (N_tracer_snaps entries) will produce a very large array — at N=1024, N//2=512 wavenumbers, and ~5000 snapshots, this is 5000×512×8 bytes ≈ 20 MB, which is fine, but the instruction should clarify that the azimuthal average is computed on the full 1024² field (not the coarsened 256² field) to preserve spectral resolution for k_peak tracking. Add this clarification explicitly.

- Step 1 (DNS simulation): The tracer advection uses "bilinear spectral interpolation" but the hardware note specifies torch.nn.functional.grid_sample for tracer interpolation. The bullet should explicitly state that grid_sample with align_corners=False and padding_mode='border' (or 'zeros' with periodic wrapping) is used, and that the velocity field is reconstructed from spectral coefficients (irfft2) before interpolation at each tracer snapshot step, to avoid ambiguity in implementation.

- Step 2 (Snapshot quality): The bullet for VACF computation should specify that C_vv(tau) is normalized by C_vv(0) (i.e., the zero-lag variance of tracer velocities), and that the computation averages over all tracers and both velocity components. Without this normalization specification, the reported C_vv(1) > 0.3 threshold is ambiguous. Also add a bullet to print the actual number of tracer snapshots saved (N_tracer_snaps) and the actual T_prod achieved, as these may differ from targets.

- Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): The McCulloch quantile method implementation must handle the case where displacement increments at a given lag may have very few samples (e.g., near the end of the production run). Add a bullet specifying a minimum sample size threshold (e.g., at least 500 displacement samples per window-lag combination) below which alpha is set to NaN rather than producing unreliable estimates. Also specify that displacement increments must be computed using periodic wrapping (mod 2pi) to avoid spurious large jumps from domain boundary crossings.

- Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): The Hill estimator instruction is underspecified. Add a bullet clarifying that the Hill estimator is applied to the absolute values of displacement increments (treating them as heavy-tailed positive random variables), that the number of order statistics k_Hill is chosen as k_Hill = floor(sqrt(N_samples)), and that the result is clipped to the range [0.5, 2.0] to avoid numerical artifacts from small samples.

- Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): The instruction to "save reconstructed fields" for j_min in {1,2,3,4} will produce large intermediate files (4 × N_vel_snaps × 2 × 256 × 256 × 4 bytes). Given the 7200s timeout, saving these to disk and reloading for tracer re-advection may be unnecessary. Instead, the bullet should specify that the filtered fields are computed on-the-fly during tracer re-advection (one j_min at a time), keeping only the current filtered field in memory, to avoid both storage and I/O bottlenecks.

- Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): The tracer re-advection in filtered fields uses "RK4 using dt=dt_snap" but the filtered velocity fields are only available at Eulerian snapshot times (dt_vel=2.0), which is 40× coarser than dt_snap=0.05. The bullet must specify that filtered velocities are linearly interpolated in time between consecutive Eulerian snapshots during the RK4 sub-steps, and that the RK4 integration uses the full dt_snap=0.05 time step with this temporal interpolation. Without this, the re-advection will be inaccurate.

- Step 7 (Okubo-Weiss analysis): The velocity gradients needed for Q = s^2 - omega^2 must be computed spectrally (using wavenumber multiplication in Fourier space) rather than finite differences on the coarsened 256² grid, to avoid numerical differentiation errors. Add a bullet specifying spectral gradient computation: load the full-resolution vorticity in spectral space if available, or compute gradients of the coarsened velocity field using 2D FFT on the 256² grid with appropriate wavenumber arrays.

- Step 9 (Results section): The researcher agent should be explicitly instructed to cross-reference the numerical values printed to console by each engineer step (Steps 2–8) and to flag any inconsistencies between the McCulloch and Hill alpha estimates (e.g., if they differ by more than 0.3), as this would indicate estimator sensitivity that must be discussed. Also add a bullet requiring the researcher to explicitly state the units of all reported physical quantities (T_eddy in simulation time units, k_peak in units of k_box=1) and to compare the final alpha values against the prior result alpha ≈ 1.4 from v1 Iteration 4.

name: reviewer_response_evaluator
content:
**Recommendations:**

Step 1 (DNS simulation): Explicitly set the forcing band to k∈[3,5] and define the spinup termination condition as T/T_L >= 10 evaluated dynamically. Require the code to print the actual T_spinup achieved and T_L at the end of spinup.

Step 1 (DNS simulation): Clarify that the azimuthal average for energy_spectrum.npy is computed on the full 1024² field to preserve spectral resolution for k_peak tracking.

Step 1 (DNS simulation): Specify that tracer interpolation uses torch.nn.functional.grid_sample with align_corners=False and padding_mode='border', and that the velocity field is reconstructed from spectral coefficients (irfft2) before interpolation.

Step 2 (Snapshot quality): Specify that C_vv(tau) is normalized by C_vv(0) and averaged over all tracers and both velocity components. Require the code to print the actual number of tracer snapshots (N_tracer_snaps) and T_prod achieved.

Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): Implement a minimum sample size threshold of 500 displacement samples per window-lag combination for the McCulloch method, setting alpha to NaN if below this threshold. Require displacement increments to be computed using periodic wrapping (mod 2pi).

Step 3 (Non-stationarity analysis): Clarify that the Hill estimator is applied to the absolute values of displacement increments, with k_Hill = floor(sqrt(N_samples)), and clip the result to the range [0.5, 2.0].

Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): Specify that filtered fields are computed on-the-fly during tracer re-advection (one j_min at a time) to avoid unnecessary disk I/O and storage bottlenecks.

Step 5 (Wavelet decomposition): Specify that filtered velocities are linearly interpolated in time between consecutive Eulerian snapshots during the RK4 sub-steps to ensure accurate re-advection.

Step 7 (Okubo-Weiss analysis): Require velocity gradients for Q = s^2 - omega^2 to be computed spectrally using 2D FFT on the 256² grid to avoid numerical differentiation errors.

Step 9 (Results section): Instruct the researcher to cross-reference console outputs, flag inconsistencies between McCulloch and Hill alpha estimates (if difference > 0.3), state units for all physical quantities, and compare final alpha values against the prior result alpha ≈ 1.4 from v1 Iteration 4.

name: review_recorder
content:
Recommendations have been logged.
Number of feedback rounds left: 0.
Now, update the plan accordingly, planner!

name: planner
content:
**Plan:**

- Step 1:
      * sub-task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
        - Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity (nu_h=1e-19, p=4, 8th-order), RK4 time integration with adaptive CFL<0.4
        - Force stochastically at k∈[3,5] (NOT k∈[1,3]) with constant energy injection rate epsilon_inj=0.1; calibrate force_amp empirically at startup by measuring actual energy injection rate and rescaling
        - Run spinup with dynamic termination condition T/T_L >= 10: at each diagnostic step compute T_L = 2*pi/U_rms from current energy spectrum, check if elapsed_time/T_L >= 10, and stop spinup only when this condition is met; save diagnostics only during spinup
        - Print after spinup: actual T_spinup achieved, T_L at end of spinup, U_rms, k_peak
        - After spinup: seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly, run production for T_prod=50*T_L_spinup_end
        - Advect tracers using torch.nn.functional.grid_sample with align_corners=False and padding_mode='border'; reconstruct velocity field from spectral coefficients via irfft2 before each interpolation step; apply periodic wrapping to tracer positions after each step
        - Save tracer positions and velocities at dt_snap=0.05; save coarsened (1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4)) Eulerian velocity and vorticity at dt_vel=2.0
        - Compute azimuthally-averaged energy spectrum E(k,t) on the full 1024² spectral field (not the coarsened field) at every tracer snapshot; save to energy_spectrum.npy
        - Save diagnostics.npy as structured array with fields (time, E_total, E_rms, omega_rms, T_L, k_peak, d_vv_estimate) at every tracer snapshot; save sim_params.json
        - All output to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/; NaN/Inf guard after each RK4 step; raise RuntimeError if triggered
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 2:
      * sub-task: Snapshot quality verification and inverse cascade confirmation
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
        - Print the actual number of tracer snapshots saved (N_tracer_snaps) and the actual T_prod achieved
        - Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step using periodic wrapping (differences mod 2pi); report whether it is below 0.3 domain units (threshold = 0.3×2π ≈ 1.885); if exceeded, print a clear WARNING message
        - Compute VACF C_vv(tau) normalized by C_vv(0), averaged over all tracers and both velocity components, at lags tau = 1,2,4,8,16 steps; report C_vv(1) and the decorrelation time tau_corr (first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e); print all values
        - Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a log-log plot to confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2; annotate k_peak at each time
        - Plot mean displacement per step and VACF vs lag in a multi-panel figure; save all plots as high-resolution PNG
        - Save verification statistics (mean displacement, C_vv values at all lags, tau_corr, N_tracer_snaps, T_prod_actual) to a json file for use in later steps

- Step 3:
      * sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) over overlapping windows
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load tracer positions/velocities, tracer_times, diagnostics from data directory; load T_L from sim_params.json
        - Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step dW=2*T_L over the production run; compute window center times
        - For each window: compute displacement increments at lags tau={0.5,1,2}*T_L using periodic wrapping (mod 2pi) to avoid spurious large jumps from domain boundary crossings
        - Apply McCulloch quantile method: compute p5,p25,p50,p75,p95 quantiles of displacement increments; compute nu_alpha = (q95-q5)/(q75-q25); interpolate from McCulloch (1986) table to get alpha; set alpha=NaN if fewer than 500 displacement samples are available for that window-lag combination
        - Apply Hill estimator as cross-check: compute on absolute values of displacement increments; use k_Hill = floor(sqrt(N_samples)) order statistics; clip result to [0.5, 2.0]; set to NaN if N_samples < 500
        - For each window: fit MSD(tau) power law (log-log linear regression) to get gamma(t); fit VACF algebraic decay (log-log linear regression) to get nu(t); extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) as window-averaged values from diagnostics
        - Perform ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on the alpha_McCulloch(t) time series (excluding NaN entries); print test statistics and p-values
        - Fit alpha vs k_peak(t)/k_box with the model alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c using nonlinear least squares; print fitted parameters and R²
        - Save all window statistics (alpha_McCulloch, alpha_Hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers) to a npz file

- Step 4:
      * sub-task: Plot non-stationarity results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load window statistics npz file saved in Step 3
        - Plot alpha(t) for both McCulloch and Hill estimators with shaded error bands vs window center time; overlay k_peak(t)/k_box on a secondary y-axis; annotate ADF and KPSS p-values as text on the plot
        - Plot gamma(t) and nu(t) vs window center time in separate panels within the same figure
        - Plot alpha vs k_peak/k_box scatter with the fitted curve alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c overlaid; annotate fitted parameters and R²
        - Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files to the data directory

- Step 5:
      * sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256), vel_times.npy, and tracer_positions.npy from data directory; extract initial tracer positions (first snapshot) as seeds
        - Apply 2D DWT (PyWavelets, wavelet='db6', mode='periodization') decomposition up to J=6 levels to each velocity snapshot; extract detail coefficients D_j={H,V,D} at each level j=1..6
        - For each scale j=1..6: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|^2 at each snapshot; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(tau,j) averaged over all spatial positions; integrate C_E trapezoidal until it drops below 0.1 to get T_eddy(j); print T_eddy values for all j
        - For each j_min in {1,2,3,4}: reconstruct filtered velocity field u_tilde_j on-the-fly (one j_min at a time, do not save all filtered fields to disk simultaneously) by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min and applying inverse DWT; keep only the current filtered field in memory
        - Re-advect tracers in each filtered field: linearly interpolate filtered velocities in time between consecutive Eulerian snapshots (at dt_vel=2.0) during RK4 sub-steps; use dt_sub=0.05 for RK4 integration; interpolate spatially using bilinear interpolation on the 256² grid; apply periodic wrapping; record tracer trajectories
        - For each filtered field j_min: compute alpha(j_min) via McCulloch method on displacement increments (min 500 samples, periodic wrapping), gamma_MSD(j_min) from MSD power law, nu_VACF(j_min) from VACF decay
        - Save T_eddy(j) for j=1..6, and alpha(j_min), gamma_MSD(j_min), nu_VACF(j_min) for j_min=1..4 to a npz file
      * code_execution_timeout: 7200

- Step 6:
      * sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load wavelet results npz (T_eddy, alpha, gamma_MSD, nu_VACF per scale j) and non-stationarity results npz (alpha_McCulloch, alpha_Hill, k_peak, window_centers) from previous steps
        - Plot alpha(j_min) vs T_eddy(j_min) with power-law fit on log-log axes; annotate the CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) with beta proportional to T_eddy^delta; report fitted delta value on the plot
        - Plot gamma_MSD(j_min) and nu_VACF(j_min) vs j_min (wavelet scale level) in separate panels
        - Construct 2D heatmap of alpha as function of (T_eddy axis from wavelet results, k_peak/k_box axis from non-stationarity results) by combining both datasets; use interpolation to fill the grid
        - Compare alpha(t) from Step 3 with alpha(j_peak(t)) from wavelet results at the dominant wavelet scale j corresponding to k_peak(t) at each time window; plot both time series on the same axes
        - Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files; save synthesis statistics (fitted delta, CTRW test results) to a npz file

- Step 7:
      * sub-task: Okubo-Weiss and LCS analysis
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256), vorticity_snapshots.npy, vel_times.npy, and tracer_positions.npy from data directory; load alpha_McCulloch and window_centers from Step 3 npz
        - Compute velocity gradients spectrally: apply 2D FFT to each 256² velocity snapshot, multiply by i*kx and i*ky wavenumber arrays, apply inverse FFT to get du/dx, du/dy, dv/dx, dv/dy; compute strain rate s^2 = (du/dx - dv/dy)^2 + (du/dy + dv/dx)^2 and vorticity squared omega^2 = (dv/dx - du/dy)^2; compute Q = s^2 - omega^2
        - Use dynamic threshold Q_threshold(t) = sigma_Q(t) (instantaneous std of Q field) at each snapshot; count hyperbolic points (Q > Q_threshold) and elliptic points (Q < -Q_threshold); record counts in time series
        - For each tracer at each snapshot: interpolate Q at tracer position; classify as trapped (Q < -Q_threshold) or flight (Q > Q_threshold); extract contiguous trapped and flight segments; compute residence time and flight time distributions
        - Tag tracers exiting vortices: classify exit as vortex death (Q at tracer position transitions from negative to positive due to vortex dissipation) vs strain stripping (tracer exits near a hyperbolic point where Q > Q_threshold in the neighborhood)
        - Compute time-lagged cross-correlation between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt (finite differences of alpha_McCulloch); report peak lag and correlation coefficient; perform Granger causality test (both directions, lag order 2) using statsmodels
        - Evaluate Pearson correlation between hyperbolic point density time series and alpha_McCulloch(t) (interpolated to matching times); print correlation coefficient and p-value
        - Save all results (Q_time_series, hyperbolic_counts, elliptic_counts, residence_times, flight_times, vortex_death_fraction, strain_stripping_fraction, cross_correlation, granger_results, pearson_r) to a npz file

- Step 8:
      * sub-task: Plot LCS, Okubo-Weiss, and causal analysis results
      * agent: engineer
      * bullet points:
        - Load LCS statistics npz from Step 7 and alpha_McCulloch from Step 3 npz
        - Plot residence time and flight time distributions on log-log axes; fit power laws to the tails; annotate fitted exponents
        - Plot hyperbolic point density vs time overlaid with alpha(t) on a dual-axis plot
        - Plot cross-correlation function between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt vs lag; annotate peak lag and peak correlation coefficient
        - Plot Granger causality F-statistics and p-values for both directions (dE→dalpha and dalpha→dE) as a bar chart
        - Plot a 2D snapshot of the Okubo-Weiss Q field at a representative mid-production time with tracer positions overlaid, colored by trapped (blue) vs flight (red) status
        - Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files to the data directory

- Step 9:
      * sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
      * agent: researcher
      * bullet points:
        - Synthesize all quantitative results from Steps 2-8 using the console outputs and saved statistics; cross-reference numerical values printed by each engineer step
        - Report snapshot quality metrics: mean displacement per step, C_vv(1), tau_corr, N_tracer_snaps, T_prod_actual; confirm whether the displacement threshold was met
        - Report inverse cascade confirmation: describe k_peak drift from ~4 toward lower wavenumbers across the production run
        - Report non-stationarity results: alpha_McCulloch(t) and alpha_Hill(t) time series with uncertainty; flag any windows where McCulloch and Hill estimates differ by more than 0.3 and discuss estimator sensitivity; report ADF and KPSS test statistics and p-values; report fitted parameters of alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c with R²
        - Report wavelet eddy-lifetime scaling: T_eddy(j) for all j, alpha(j_min) for all j_min, fitted power-law exponent delta in beta proportional to T_eddy^delta; state whether CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) is supported
        - Report LCS/Okubo-Weiss statistics: vortex death fraction vs strain stripping fraction; residence time and flight time power-law exponents; hyperbolic point density correlation with alpha(t)
        - Report causal analysis: peak lag and correlation coefficient of cross-correlation between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt; Granger causality F-statistics and p-values for both directions
        - State units for all reported physical quantities (T_eddy in simulation time units, k_peak in units of k_box=1, alpha dimensionless)
        - Compare final alpha values against prior result alpha ≈ 1.4 from v1 Iteration 4; discuss whether alpha is stationary or non-stationary and whether spectral condensation drives the evolution of Lagrangian geometry
        - Structure the Results section with subsections: (1) Simulation Quality and Inverse Cascade, (2) Non-Stationary Lévy Analysis, (3) Wavelet Eddy-Lifetime Scaling, (4) LCS and Okubo-Weiss Analysis, (5) Causal

name: planner_response_evaluator
content:
**PLAN**

- Step 1:
	* sub-task: DNS simulation — 2D Navier-Stokes solver with tracer advection
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Implement GPU-accelerated pseudo-spectral 2D NS solver in vorticity-streamfunction form on N=1024 grid using PyTorch (device='cuda'), with torch.fft.rfft2/irfft2
		- Use integrating factor (IF) method for hyperviscosity (nu_h=1e-19, p=4, 8th-order), RK4 time integration with adaptive CFL<0.4
		- Force stochastically at k∈[3,5] (NOT k∈[1,3]) with constant energy injection rate epsilon_inj=0.1; calibrate force_amp empirically at startup by measuring actual energy injection rate and rescaling
		- Run spinup with dynamic termination condition T/T_L >= 10: at each diagnostic step compute T_L = 2*pi/U_rms from current energy spectrum, check if elapsed_time/T_L >= 10, and stop spinup only when this condition is met; save diagnostics only during spinup
		- Print after spinup: actual T_spinup achieved, T_L at end of spinup, U_rms, k_peak
		- After spinup: seed N_tracers=5000 uniformly, run production for T_prod=50*T_L_spinup_end
		- Advect tracers using torch.nn.functional.grid_sample with align_corners=False and padding_mode='border'; reconstruct velocity field from spectral coefficients via irfft2 before each interpolation step; apply periodic wrapping to tracer positions after each step
		- Save tracer positions and velocities at dt_snap=0.05; save coarsened (1024→256 via AvgPool2d(4)) Eulerian velocity and vorticity at dt_vel=2.0
		- Compute azimuthally-averaged energy spectrum E(k,t) on the full 1024² spectral field (not the coarsened field) at every tracer snapshot; save to energy_spectrum.npy
		- Save diagnostics.npy as structured array with fields (time, E_total, E_rms, omega_rms, T_L, k_peak, d_vv_estimate) at every tracer snapshot; save sim_params.json
		- All output to /home/node/work/projects/levy_flights_2dns_v2/data/; NaN/Inf guard after each RK4 step; raise RuntimeError if triggered

- Step 2:
	* sub-task: Snapshot quality verification and inverse cascade confirmation
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer_positions.npy, tracer_velocities.npy, tracer_times.npy, energy_spectrum.npy, diagnostics.npy from the data directory
		- Print the actual number of tracer snapshots saved (N_tracer_snaps) and the actual T_prod achieved
		- Compute mean tracer displacement per snapshot step using periodic wrapping (differences mod 2pi); report whether it is below 0.3 domain units (threshold = 0.3×2π ≈ 1.885); if exceeded, print a clear WARNING message
		- Compute VACF C_vv(tau) normalized by C_vv(0), averaged over all tracers and both velocity components, at lags tau = 1,2,4,8,16 steps; report C_vv(1) and the decorrelation time tau_corr (first lag where C_vv drops below 1/e); print all values
		- Plot E(k) at t = 0.1, 0.3, 0.6, 1.0 × T_prod on a log-log plot to confirm k_peak drifts from ~4 toward ~1-2; annotate k_peak at each time
		- Plot mean displacement per step and VACF vs lag in a multi-panel figure; save all plots as high-resolution PNG
		- Save verification statistics (mean displacement, C_vv values at all lags, tau_corr, N_tracer_snaps, T_prod_actual) to a json file for use in later steps

- Step 3:
	* sub-task: Non-stationarity analysis — α(t), γ(t), ν(t), k_peak(t) over overlapping windows
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load tracer positions/velocities, tracer_times, diagnostics from data directory; load T_L from sim_params.json
		- Define overlapping windows of width W=8*T_L, step dW=2*T_L over the production run; compute window center times
		- For each window: compute displacement increments at lags tau={0.5,1,2}*T_L using periodic wrapping (mod 2pi) to avoid spurious large jumps from domain boundary crossings
		- Apply McCulloch quantile method: compute p5,p25,p50,p75,p95 quantiles of displacement increments; compute nu_alpha = (q95-q5)/(q75-q25); interpolate from McCulloch (1986) table to get alpha; set alpha=NaN if fewer than 500 displacement samples are available for that window-lag combination
		- Apply Hill estimator as cross-check: compute on absolute values of displacement increments; use k_Hill = floor(sqrt(N_samples)) order statistics; clip result to [0.5, 2.0]; set to NaN if N_samples < 500
		- For each window: fit MSD(tau) power law (log-log linear regression) to get gamma(t); fit VACF algebraic decay (log-log linear regression) to get nu(t); extract d_vv(t) and k_peak(t) as window-averaged values from diagnostics
		- Perform ADF and KPSS stationarity tests on the alpha_McCulloch(t) time series (excluding NaN entries); print test statistics and p-values
		- Fit alpha vs k_peak(t)/k_box with the model alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c using nonlinear least squares; print fitted parameters and R²
		- Save all window statistics (alpha_McCulloch, alpha_Hill, gamma, nu, d_vv, k_peak, window_centers) to a npz file

- Step 4:
	* sub-task: Plot non-stationarity results
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load window statistics npz file saved in Step 3
		- Plot alpha(t) for both McCulloch and Hill estimators with shaded error bands vs window center time; overlay k_peak(t)/k_box on a secondary y-axis; annotate ADF and KPSS p-values as text on the plot
		- Plot gamma(t) and nu(t) vs window center time in separate panels within the same figure
		- Plot alpha vs k_peak/k_box scatter with the fitted curve alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c overlaid; annotate fitted parameters and R²
		- Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files to the data directory

- Step 5:
	* sub-task: Wavelet decomposition, eddy-lifetime estimation, and filtered tracer re-advection
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* code_execution_timeout: 7200s

	* instructions:
		- Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256), vel_times.npy, and tracer_positions.npy from data directory; extract initial tracer positions (first snapshot) as seeds
		- Apply 2D DWT (PyWavelets, wavelet='db6', mode='periodization') decomposition up to J=6 levels to each velocity snapshot; extract detail coefficients D_j={H,V,D} at each level j=1..6
		- For each scale j=1..6: compute wavelet energy E_j(x,y,t) = sum_d |D_j^d(x,y,t)|^2 at each snapshot; compute temporal autocorrelation C_E(tau,j) averaged over all spatial positions; integrate C_E trapezoidal until it drops below 0.1 to get T_eddy(j); print T_eddy values for all j
		- For each j_min in {1,2,3,4}: reconstruct filtered velocity field u_tilde_j on-the-fly (one j_min at a time, do not save all filtered fields to disk simultaneously) by zeroing detail coefficients below j_min and applying inverse DWT; keep only the current filtered field in memory
		- Re-advect tracers in each filtered field: linearly interpolate filtered velocities in time between consecutive Eulerian snapshots (at dt_vel=2.0) during RK4 sub-steps; use dt_sub=0.05 for RK4 integration; interpolate spatially using bilinear interpolation on the 256² grid; apply periodic wrapping; record tracer trajectories
		- For each filtered field j_min: compute alpha(j_min) via McCulloch method on displacement increments (min 500 samples, periodic wrapping), gamma_MSD(j_min) from MSD power law, nu_VACF(j_min) from VACF decay
		- Save T_eddy(j) for j=1..6, and alpha(j_min), gamma_MSD(j_min), nu_VACF(j_min) for j_min=1..4 to a npz file

- Step 6:
	* sub-task: Plot wavelet scaling results and synthesis
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load wavelet results npz (T_eddy, alpha, gamma_MSD, nu_VACF per scale j) and non-stationarity results npz (alpha_McCulloch, alpha_Hill, k_peak, window_centers) from previous steps
		- Plot alpha(j_min) vs T_eddy(j_min) with power-law fit on log-log axes; annotate the CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) with beta proportional to T_eddy^delta; report fitted delta value on the plot
		- Plot gamma_MSD(j_min) and nu_VACF(j_min) vs j_min (wavelet scale level) in separate panels
		- Construct 2D heatmap of alpha as function of (T_eddy axis from wavelet results, k_peak/k_box axis from non-stationarity results) by combining both datasets; use interpolation to fill the grid
		- Compare alpha(t) from Step 3 with alpha(j_peak(t)) from wavelet results at the dominant wavelet scale j corresponding to k_peak(t) at each time window; plot both time series on the same axes
		- Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files; save synthesis statistics (fitted delta, CTRW test results) to a npz file

- Step 7:
	* sub-task: Okubo-Weiss and LCS analysis
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load velocity_snapshots.npy (shape: N_vel_snaps, 2, 256, 256), vorticity_snapshots.npy, vel_times.npy, and tracer_positions.npy from data directory; load alpha_McCulloch and window_centers from Step 3 npz
		- Compute velocity gradients spectrally: apply 2D FFT to each 256² velocity snapshot, multiply by i*kx and i*ky wavenumber arrays, apply inverse FFT to get du/dx, du/dy, dv/dx, dv/dy; compute strain rate s^2 = (du/dx - dv/dy)^2 + (du/dy + dv/dx)^2 and vorticity squared omega^2 = (dv/dx - du/dy)^2; compute Q = s^2 - omega^2
		- Use dynamic threshold Q_threshold(t) = sigma_Q(t) (instantaneous std of Q field) at each snapshot; count hyperbolic points (Q > Q_threshold) and elliptic points (Q < -Q_threshold); record counts in time series
		- For each tracer at each snapshot: interpolate Q at tracer position; classify as trapped (Q < -Q_threshold) or flight (Q > Q_threshold); extract contiguous trapped and flight segments; compute residence time and flight time distributions
		- Tag tracers exiting vortices: classify exit as vortex death (Q at tracer position transitions from negative to positive due to vortex dissipation) vs strain stripping (tracer exits near a hyperbolic point where Q > Q_threshold in the neighborhood)
		- Compute time-lagged cross-correlation between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt (finite differences of alpha_McCulloch); report peak lag and correlation coefficient; perform Granger causality test (both directions, lag order 2) using statsmodels
		- Evaluate Pearson correlation between hyperbolic point density time series and alpha_McCulloch(t) (interpolated to matching times); print correlation coefficient and p-value
		- Save all results (Q_time_series, hyperbolic_counts, elliptic_counts, residence_times, flight_times, vortex_death_fraction, strain_stripping_fraction, cross_correlation, granger_results, pearson_r) to a npz file

- Step 8:
	* sub-task: Plot LCS, Okubo-Weiss, and causal analysis results
	* agent in charge: engineer

	* instructions:
		- Load LCS statistics npz from Step 7 and alpha_McCulloch from Step 3 npz
		- Plot residence time and flight time distributions on log-log axes; fit power laws to the tails; annotate fitted exponents
		- Plot hyperbolic point density vs time overlaid with alpha(t) on a dual-axis plot
		- Plot cross-correlation function between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt vs lag; annotate peak lag and peak correlation coefficient
		- Plot Granger causality F-statistics and p-values for both directions (dE→dalpha and dalpha→dE) as a bar chart
		- Plot a 2D snapshot of the Okubo-Weiss Q field at a representative mid-production time with tracer positions overlaid, colored by trapped (blue) vs flight (red) status
		- Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files to the data directory

- Step 9:
	* sub-task: Write full Results section of the paper
	* agent in charge: researcher

	* instructions:
		- Synthesize all quantitative results from Steps 2-8 using the console outputs and saved statistics; cross-reference numerical values printed by each engineer step
		- Report snapshot quality metrics: mean displacement per step, C_vv(1), tau_corr, N_tracer_snaps, T_prod_actual; confirm whether the displacement threshold was met
		- Report inverse cascade confirmation: describe k_peak drift from ~4 toward lower wavenumbers across the production run
		- Report non-stationarity results: alpha_McCulloch(t) and alpha_Hill(t) time series with uncertainty; flag any windows where McCulloch and Hill estimates differ by more than 0.3 and discuss estimator sensitivity; report ADF and KPSS test statistics and p-values; report fitted parameters of alpha = a + b*(k_peak/k_box)^c with R²
		- Report wavelet eddy-lifetime scaling: T_eddy(j) for all j, alpha(j_min) for all j_min, fitted power-law exponent delta in beta proportional to T_eddy^delta; state whether CTRW prediction alpha=1+beta/(2-beta) is supported
		- Report LCS/Okubo-Weiss statistics: vortex death fraction vs strain stripping fraction; residence time and flight time power-law exponents; hyperbolic point density correlation with alpha(t)
		- Report causal analysis: peak lag and correlation coefficient of cross-correlation between dE(k_min)/dt and d_alpha/dt; Granger causality F-statistics and p-values for both directions
		- State units for all reported physical quantities (T_eddy in simulation time units, k_peak in units of k_box=1, alpha dimensionless)
		- Compare final alpha values against prior result alpha ≈ 1.4 from v1 Iteration 4; discuss whether alpha is stationary or non-stationary and whether spectral condensation drives the evolution of Lagrangian geometry
		- Structure the Results section with subsections: (1) Simulation Quality and Inverse Cascade, (2) Non-Stationary Lévy Analysis, (3) Wavelet Eddy-Lifetime Scaling, (4) LCS and Okubo-Weiss Analysis, (5) Causal

name: plan_recorder
content: Plan recorded as final. Planning stage complete.
